title: Epic: Enrich SDK lexicons for GainForest biodiversity monitoring platform
description:
## Context
GainForest is migrating entirely to ATProto. The climateai-sdk-scalability lexicons are the canonical source but are missing critical fields needed by the Green Globe app and GainForest's biodiversity monitoring, data analytics, and data sovereignty mission.

## Current State
- The SDK has good foundations: Darwin Core occurrence/event/measurement records, evaluator system, common media defs, organization info/site/defaultSite
- The old organization-level observations/predictions lexicons (flora.json, fauna.json) are correctly marked DEPRECATED — they only store GBIF taxon key arrays with no location, evidence, measurements, or provenance
- The Green Globe app currently pulls rich biodiversity data from AWS S3 (Restor JSON for plants with traits/IUCN/images, CSV for animals, GeoJSON for measured trees with per-tree measurements and photos)
- Organization info lexicon is missing fields the app needs (contact info, social links, funding, team size, ecosystem type)
- Site lexicon is missing environmental context (biome, elevation, climate, protection status)
- Audio recordings lexicon exists but needs bioacoustics-specific fields
- No lexicon exists for community members, dynamic map layers, or financial transparency
- The DwC occurrence record is comprehensive but could use a few GainForest-specific optional fields

## Goals
1. Enrich existing SDK lexicons with optional fields that make them useful for GainForest's specific needs
2. Add missing lexicons for data currently sourced from AWS S3, Airtable, old GraphQL API, and backend API
3. Ensure all lexicons support data sovereignty (provenance, licensing, evidence)
4. Maintain backward compatibility — all new fields are OPTIONAL
5. All changes happen in climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/

## Scope
- ~12 lexicon files to modify or create in climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/
- No code changes — lexicon JSON schema files only
- No changes to existing required fields

title: Enrich organization info lexicon with operational and contact fields
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/info.json (modify)

## What to do
Add the following OPTIONAL properties to the existing record schema (do NOT modify any existing required fields or properties):

### Contact & Social
- `email` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 256) — contact email
- `socialLinks` (array of objects, maxLength: 10) — each with `platform` (string, knownValues: ['twitter', 'instagram', 'facebook', 'linkedin', 'youtube', 'tiktok', 'github', 'discord', 'telegram', 'other'], maxGraphemes: 64) and `url` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512)
- `discordId` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — Discord server ID
- `stripeUrl` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512) — donation link

### Operational
- `teamSize` (integer, minimum: 1) — number of team members
- `foundedYear` (integer, minimum: 1900, maximum: 2100) — year organization was founded
- `ecosystemTypes` (array of strings, maxLength: 10, knownValues: ['tropical-rainforest', 'temperate-forest', 'boreal-forest', 'mangrove', 'coral-reef', 'savanna', 'grassland', 'wetland', 'desert', 'alpine', 'marine', 'freshwater', 'urban', 'agroforestry', 'other']) — types of ecosystems the org works in
- `focusSpeciesGroups` (array of strings, maxLength: 20, knownValues: ['mammals', 'birds', 'reptiles', 'amphibians', 'fish', 'insects', 'arachnids', 'mollusks', 'crustaceans', 'trees', 'shrubs', 'herbs', 'grasses', 'ferns', 'mosses', 'fungi', 'algae', 'coral', 'other']) — taxonomic groups the org focuses on

### Data & Transparency
- `dataLicense` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — default license for data (e.g., CC-BY-4.0)
- `dataDownloadUrl` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512) — URL to download org's data
- `dataDownloadInfo` (string, maxGraphemes: 1024) — description of available data downloads
- `fundingSourcesDescription` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048) — description of funding sources

### Define socialLinks items as a separate def called `socialLink` in the same file's defs section.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any existing required fields
- Do NOT change the id, key, or description of the main record
- Do NOT add any new required fields — everything must be optional
- Do NOT remove or rename any existing properties
- Do NOT change the visibility enum values (keep Public/Unlisted as-is)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. All new fields are optional (not in required array)
3. All existing fields are unchanged (diff shows only additions)
4. socialLink def is properly defined in defs section
5. socialLinks items reference the socialLink def correctly
6. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
7. All integer fields have minimum/maximum constraints where appropriate
8. knownValues arrays are used (not enum) for extensible lists
close_reason: 8ece85b Enrich organization info lexicon with operational and contact fields

title: Create donation/transaction lexicon for financial transparency
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/donation.json (create)

## What to do
Create a new lexicon for recording donations and financial transactions. Currently this data comes from AWS S3 CSV files (fiat transactions) and blockchain APIs (Celo, Solana). This lexicon enables financial transparency on-chain via ATProto.

### Lexicon id: app.gainforest.organization.donation
### Record key: tid

### Required fields:
- `amount` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — donation amount as string (e.g., '100.00')
- `currency` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'CELO', 'SOL')
- `donatedAt` (string, format: datetime) — when the donation was made
- `createdAt` (string, format: datetime) — record creation timestamp

### Optional fields:
- `donorName` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — donor name (may be anonymous)
- `donorDid` (string, format: did) — donor's ATProto DID if they have an account
- `recipientMemberRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI to the member record who received funds
- `transactionType` (string, maxGraphemes: 32, knownValues: ['fiat', 'crypto', 'grant', 'in-kind', 'other'])
- `paymentMethod` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['stripe', 'bank-transfer', 'celo', 'solana', 'ethereum', 'polygon', 'paypal', 'other'])
- `transactionHash` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — blockchain transaction hash if crypto
- `blockchainNetwork` (string, maxGraphemes: 32, knownValues: ['celo', 'solana', 'ethereum', 'polygon', 'other'])
- `purpose` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — what the donation is for (e.g., 'tree planting', 'equipment', 'salaries')
- `isAnonymous` (boolean) — whether the donor wishes to remain anonymous
- `receiptUrl` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512) — URL to donation receipt
- `notes` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048) — additional notes
- `amountUsd` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — equivalent amount in USD at time of donation

## Don't
- Do NOT store private financial details (bank account numbers, card numbers)
- Do NOT make donor identity fields required (donations can be anonymous)
- Do NOT use enum for payment methods — use knownValues for extensibility
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. Lexicon id is exactly 'app.gainforest.organization.donation'
3. Record key is 'tid'
4. Required array contains exactly: amount, currency, donatedAt, createdAt
5. All other fields are optional
6. donorDid has format: did
7. recipientMemberRef has format: at-uri
8. transactionType, paymentMethod, blockchainNetwork use knownValues (not enum)
9. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
10. File path is climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/donation.json
close_reason: 9839651 feat: add donation/transaction lexicon for financial transparency

title: Enrich measuredTreesCluster lexicon with cluster-level metadata
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/observations/measuredTreesCluster.json (modify)

## What to do
The existing measuredTreesCluster lexicon only stores a shapefile blob and createdAt. The Green Globe app displays rich per-cluster data (species counts, height distributions, measurement dates). Add optional fields for cluster-level summary metadata.

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

#### Cluster Identity
- `name` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — name of the tree cluster/plot (e.g., 'Plot A - Riparian Zone')
- `description` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext) — description of the cluster
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the site this cluster belongs to

#### Location
- `decimalLatitude` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — centroid latitude
- `decimalLongitude` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — centroid longitude
- `areaSqMeters` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — area of the cluster in square meters

#### Summary Statistics
- `totalTreeCount` (integer, minimum: 0) — total number of measured trees
- `speciesCount` (integer, minimum: 0) — number of distinct species
- `averageHeightMeters` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — average tree height
- `averageDbhCm` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — average diameter at breast height in cm
- `dominantSpecies` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — most common species scientific name
- `measurementDateRange` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — date range of measurements (ISO 8601 interval)

#### Data Provenance
- `measuredBy` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — person(s) who measured (pipe-delimited)
- `measurementProtocol` (string, maxGraphemes: 1024) — description of measurement protocol
- `dataSource` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — source of the data (e.g., 'KoBoToolbox', 'field survey')
- `license` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — data license

## Don't
- Do NOT change existing required fields (shapefile, createdAt)
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT add any new required fields
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. Existing required fields unchanged: shapefile, createdAt
3. All new fields are optional
4. description refs app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext
5. siteRef has format: at-uri
6. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
7. All integer fields have minimum: 0
8. shapefile still refs org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob
close_reason: 8d7f81f Enrich measuredTreesCluster lexicon with cluster-level metadata

title: Enrich dendogram lexicon with phylogenetic analysis metadata
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/observations/dendogram.json (modify)

## What to do
The existing dendogram lexicon only stores an SVG blob and createdAt. Add optional fields that provide context about the phylogenetic analysis — what species are included, what method was used, and how to interpret the visualization.

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

#### Analysis Context
- `name` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — name/title of the dendogram (e.g., 'Flora Phylogenetic Tree - Site A 2025')
- `description` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext) — description of what this dendogram shows
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the site this dendogram represents
- `analysisDate` (string, format: datetime) — when the analysis was performed
- `analysisMethod` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — method used to generate the dendogram (e.g., 'Maximum Likelihood with RAxML', 'Neighbor-Joining')
- `dataSource` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — source of the sequence/trait data

#### Content Summary
- `taxonCount` (integer, minimum: 0) — number of taxa in the dendogram
- `taxonGroup` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['flora', 'fauna', 'fungi', 'mixed']) — which taxonomic group
- `rootTaxon` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — the root taxon of the tree (e.g., 'Plantae')
- `treeType` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['phylogenetic', 'phenetic', 'cladistic', 'functional-trait', 'other']) — type of tree

#### Display
- `thumbnail` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail) — thumbnail preview

## Don't
- Do NOT change existing required fields (dendogram, createdAt)
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT add any new required fields
- Do NOT rename 'dendogram' to 'dendrogram' (keep existing spelling for backward compat)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. Existing required fields unchanged: dendogram, createdAt
3. All new fields are optional
4. description refs app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext
5. thumbnail refs app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail
6. siteRef has format: at-uri
7. analysisDate has format: datetime
8. taxonGroup and treeType use knownValues (not enum)
9. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
10. Original 'dendogram' spelling preserved
close_reason: 4e5ab2e Enrich dendogram lexicon with phylogenetic analysis metadata

title: Enrich evaluator defs with GainForest-specific result types
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/evaluator/defs.json (modify)
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/evaluator/evaluation.json (modify)

## What to do
Add new evaluation result types to the evaluator system that support GainForest's specific AI/ML pipelines: bioacoustics species detection, deforestation alerts, carbon estimation, and habitat quality assessment.

### New defs to add to evaluator/defs.json:

#### `bioacousticsResult`
Result of audio-based species detection. Required: `detections`. Properties:
- `detections` (array of refs to #bioacousticsDetection, maxLength: 100) — detected species/sounds
- `totalDurationAnalyzedSeconds` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — total audio duration analyzed
- `soundscapeIndex` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — acoustic diversity index value
- `remarks` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048)

#### `bioacousticsDetection`
A single detection within an audio recording. Required: `startTimeSeconds`, `endTimeSeconds`. Properties:
- `startTimeSeconds` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — start time in seconds from recording start
- `endTimeSeconds` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — end time in seconds
- `minFrequencyHz` (integer, minimum: 0) — lower frequency bound of detection
- `maxFrequencyHz` (integer, minimum: 0) — upper frequency bound of detection
- `scientificName` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — identified species
- `commonName` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — common name
- `confidence` (integer, minimum: 0, maximum: 1000) — confidence (0-1000)
- `soundType` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['call', 'song', 'alarm', 'drumming', 'echolocation', 'stridulation', 'anthropogenic', 'geophony', 'unknown'])

#### `deforestationResult`
Deforestation/land-use change detection result. Required: `changeType`. Properties:
- `changeType` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['deforestation', 'degradation', 'reforestation', 'afforestation', 'no-change', 'fire', 'flooding', 'urbanization'])
- `areaAffectedHectares` (string, maxGraphemes: 32)
- `changePercentage` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — percentage of monitored area affected
- `detectionDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — ISO 8601 date of detection
- `baselineDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — ISO 8601 date of baseline comparison
- `satelliteSource` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — satellite data source (e.g., 'Sentinel-2', 'Landsat-8', 'Planet')
- `remarks` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048)

#### `carbonEstimationResult`
Carbon stock or sequestration estimation. Required: `estimationType`, `value`, `unit`. Properties:
- `estimationType` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['above-ground-biomass', 'below-ground-biomass', 'soil-carbon', 'total-carbon-stock', 'annual-sequestration', 'avoided-emissions'])
- `value` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — estimated value
- `unit` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — unit (e.g., 'tCO2e', 'tC/ha', 'tCO2e/year')
- `uncertainty` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — uncertainty range (e.g., '±15%')
- `methodology` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — estimation methodology
- `remarks` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048)

### Update evaluator/evaluation.json:
Add the 3 new result types to the `result` union refs array:
- `app.gainforest.evaluator.defs#bioacousticsResult`
- `app.gainforest.evaluator.defs#deforestationResult`
- `app.gainforest.evaluator.defs#carbonEstimationResult`

## Don't
- Do NOT modify any existing defs in evaluator/defs.json
- Do NOT remove any existing refs from the evaluation.json result union
- Do NOT change any required fields in evaluation.json
acceptance_criteria:
1. Both files are valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schemas
2. All existing defs in evaluator/defs.json unchanged
3. 4 new defs added: bioacousticsResult, bioacousticsDetection, deforestationResult, carbonEstimationResult
4. bioacousticsResult.detections refs #bioacousticsDetection
5. evaluation.json result union contains all 5 original refs PLUS 3 new refs
6. All existing evaluation.json required fields unchanged (evaluationType, createdAt)
7. All knownValues used (not enum) for extensible lists
8. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
9. All integer fields have appropriate minimum/maximum
10. bioacousticsDetection.confidence has minimum: 0, maximum: 1000
close_reason: 3104dbb Add bioacoustics, deforestation, and carbon estimation result types to evaluator lexicons

title: Fix: Re-implement info.json enrichment — all fields missing from task 1.1
description:

A new def called `socialLink` with type: object, required: ['platform', 'url'], properties:

title: Fix: Refactor occurrence.json to use shared def refs instead of inline duplication
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.json (modify)

## Context
Tasks 1.6, 1.7, and 1.8 ran in parallel. Task 1.7 created `app.gainforest.common.defs#conservationStatus` and task 1.8 created `app.gainforest.dwc.defs#plantTraits`. But task 1.6 added the same fields inline on occurrence.json instead of referencing those shared defs. This creates duplication and naming inconsistencies.

## What to do

### Step 1: Replace inline conservation fields with a ref
Remove these 6 individual properties from occurrence.json:
- `iucnCategory`
- `iucnTaxonId`
- `iucnAssessmentDate`
- `nativeStatus`
- `citesAppendix`
- `nationalConservationStatus`

Replace them with a single property:
- `conservationStatus` (type: ref, ref: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#conservationStatus', description: 'Conservation status information including IUCN category, CITES listing, and native/invasive status.')

### Step 2: Replace inline plant trait fields with a ref
Remove these 8 individual properties from occurrence.json:
- `woodDensity`
- `treeMaxHeight`
- `stemDiameter`
- `stemConduitDiameter`
- `barkThickness`
- `rootDepth`
- `edibleParts`
- `economicUses`

Replace them with a single property:
- `plantTraits` (type: ref, ref: 'app.gainforest.dwc.defs#plantTraits', description: 'Functional plant traits from databases like TRY and Restor. Only applicable to flora occurrences.')

### Step 3: Keep the remaining fields
These fields from task 1.6 should stay as-is (they are NOT duplicated in any shared def):
- `projectRef`
- `siteRef`
- `monitoringProgramme`
- `datasetRef`
- `thumbnailUrl`
- `speciesImageUrl`

### Validation step
After modifying the file, verify it is valid by running:
```
echo y | npx @atproto/lex-cli gen-api ./test-lex-api ./lexicons/* ./lexicons/*/* ./lexicons/*/*/* ./lexicons/*/*/*/* ./lexicons/*/*/*/*/* ./lexicons/*/*/*/*/*/* 2>&1
```
from the `climateai-sdk-scalability` directory. Then delete the `test-lex-api` directory. If gen-api fails, fix the JSON and retry.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any existing required fields (basisOfRecord, scientificName, eventDate, createdAt)
- Do NOT remove projectRef, siteRef, monitoringProgramme, datasetRef, thumbnailUrl, speciesImageUrl
- Do NOT modify any pre-existing fields from the original occurrence.json (before task 1.6)
- Do NOT modify the shared defs files (common/defs.json or dwc/defs.json)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON — confirmed by successful @atproto/lex-cli gen-api run
2. All existing required fields unchanged: basisOfRecord, scientificName, eventDate, createdAt
3. No inline conservation fields remain (iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, iucnAssessmentDate, nativeStatus, citesAppendix, nationalConservationStatus are all removed)
4. A single conservationStatus ref property exists pointing to app.gainforest.common.defs#conservationStatus
5. No inline plant trait fields remain (woodDensity, treeMaxHeight, stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, edibleParts, economicUses are all removed)
6. A single plantTraits ref property exists pointing to app.gainforest.dwc.defs#plantTraits
7. projectRef, siteRef, monitoringProgramme, datasetRef, thumbnailUrl, speciesImageUrl are still present
8. conservationStatus and plantTraits are optional (not in required array)
9. test-lex-api directory is deleted after validation
close_reason: 0619d89 Refactor occurrence.json to use shared def refs instead of inline duplication

title: Fix: Add missing maxGraphemes constraints to layer.json pre-existing fields
description:
## Files

title: Create organization site lexicon with environmental context fields
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/site.json (create)

## What to do
The SDK is missing a site lexicon entirely (it exists in the green_globe project but not in the SDK). Create a new site lexicon at the path above based on the existing green_globe version but enriched with environmental context.

### Required fields (keep from existing):
- `name` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — site name
- `lat` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — latitude of centerpoint
- `lon` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — longitude of centerpoint
- `area` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — area in hectares
- `shapefile` (ref to org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob) — GeoJSON boundary blob
- `createdAt` (string, format: datetime)

### New OPTIONAL fields to add:

#### Location Context
- `country` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — country name
- `countryCode` (string, minLength: 2, maxLength: 2) — ISO 3166-1 alpha-2
- `stateProvince` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — first-level admin division
- `locality` (string, maxGraphemes: 1024) — specific locality description
- `minimumElevationInMeters` (integer) — lower elevation bound
- `maximumElevationInMeters` (integer) — upper elevation bound

#### Environmental Context
- `biome` (string, maxGraphemes: 128, knownValues: ['tropical-moist-forest', 'tropical-dry-forest', 'temperate-broadleaf-forest', 'temperate-conifer-forest', 'boreal-forest', 'tropical-grassland-savanna', 'temperate-grassland', 'flooded-grassland', 'montane-grassland', 'tundra', 'mediterranean-forest', 'desert-xeric-shrubland', 'mangrove', 'coral-reef', 'freshwater', 'marine', 'other'])
- `ecosystemType` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — freeform ecosystem description
- `protectionStatus` (string, maxGraphemes: 128, knownValues: ['national-park', 'nature-reserve', 'wildlife-sanctuary', 'biosphere-reserve', 'world-heritage', 'ramsar-site', 'community-conserved', 'indigenous-territory', 'private-reserve', 'buffer-zone', 'unprotected', 'other'])
- `iucnProtectedAreaCategory` (string, maxGraphemes: 16, knownValues: ['Ia', 'Ib', 'II', 'III', 'IV', 'V', 'VI'])
- `wdpaId` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — World Database on Protected Areas ID
- `averageAnnualRainfallMm` (integer, minimum: 0) — average annual rainfall in mm
- `averageTemperatureCelsius` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — average annual temperature
- `climatezone` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — Koppen climate classification

#### Monitoring Context
- `monitoringStartDate` (string, format: datetime) — when monitoring began
- `description` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext) — site description
- `boundary` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512) — URL to boundary GeoJSON (alternative to blob)
- `siteRemarks` (string, maxGraphemes: 5000) — additional notes

### Record key: tid
### Lexicon id: app.gainforest.organization.site

## Don't
- Do NOT use `enum` for extensible value lists — use `knownValues` instead
- Do NOT make any new fields required — only the 6 fields listed under Required
- Do NOT change the lexicon id from app.gainforest.organization.site
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. Lexicon id is exactly 'app.gainforest.organization.site'
3. Record key is 'tid'
4. Required array contains exactly: name, lat, lon, area, shapefile, createdAt
5. All new environmental/location/monitoring fields are optional
6. shapefile refs org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob
7. description refs app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext
8. knownValues used (not enum) for biome, protectionStatus, iucnProtectedAreaCategory
9. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
10. File path is climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/site.json
close_reason: 3104dbb Add enriched organization site lexicon with environmental context

title: Enrich audio recordings lexicon with bioacoustics metadata
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/recordings/audio.json (modify)

## What to do
The existing audio recording lexicon has basic metadata (codec, channels, duration, recordedAt, sampleRate, coordinates). Add optional fields critical for bioacoustics monitoring — GainForest's core use case for audio.

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the `metadata` object:

#### Recording Context
- `deviceModel` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — recording device model (e.g., 'AudioMoth 1.2.0', 'Song Meter SM4')
- `deviceSerialNumber` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — device serial number for tracking
- `gain` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — gain setting (e.g., 'medium', '36dB')
- `bitDepth` (integer, minimum: 8, maximum: 64) — bits per sample (e.g., 16, 24, 32)
- `fileFormat` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — file format (e.g., 'WAV', 'FLAC', 'MP3')
- `fileSizeBytes` (integer, minimum: 0) — file size in bytes

#### Location
- `decimalLatitude` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — latitude in decimal degrees (WGS84)
- `decimalLongitude` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — longitude in decimal degrees (WGS84)
- `altitude` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — altitude in meters
- `habitat` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — habitat description
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the organization site record

#### Bioacoustics Analysis
- `minFrequencyHz` (integer, minimum: 0) — minimum frequency in recording (Hz)
- `maxFrequencyHz` (integer, minimum: 0) — maximum frequency in recording (Hz)
- `signalToNoiseRatio` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — SNR in dB
- `weatherConditions` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — weather during recording
- `temperature` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — temperature in Celsius during recording
- `humidity` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — relative humidity percentage
- `windSpeed` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — wind speed during recording

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

- `spectrogram` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#spectrogram) — spectrogram image of the recording
- `thumbnail` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail) — thumbnail image for display
- `license` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — license for the recording (e.g., CC-BY-4.0)
- `recordedBy` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — person(s) who made the recording
- `tags` (array of strings, maxLength: 20, each maxGraphemes: 64) — freeform tags (e.g., 'dawn-chorus', 'rain', 'chainsaw')

### Also replace the existing `coordinates` field in metadata:
Keep the existing `coordinates` field as-is for backward compatibility, but add the new `decimalLatitude`/`decimalLongitude`/`altitude` fields as the preferred alternative (add a description note on coordinates saying 'Deprecated: prefer decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude fields').

## Don't
- Do NOT remove or rename any existing required fields or properties
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT add any new required fields
- Do NOT remove the existing coordinates field
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. All existing required fields unchanged (name, blob, metadata, createdAt in main; codec, channels, duration, recordedAt, sampleRate in metadata)
3. All new fields are optional
4. spectrogram refs app.gainforest.common.defs#spectrogram
5. thumbnail refs app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail
6. Existing coordinates field preserved with deprecation note in description
7. New decimalLatitude/decimalLongitude fields added to metadata
8. siteRef has format: at-uri
9. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
10. All integer fields have appropriate minimum/maximum
close_reason: 3104dbb Enrich audio recordings lexicon with bioacoustics metadata

title: Enrich layer lexicon with visualization and legend metadata
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/layer.json (modify)

## What to do
The existing layer lexicon has name, type, uri, description, createdAt. The Green Globe app currently loads layer configs from AWS S3 JSON files with richer metadata. Add optional fields to support the full layer visualization pipeline.

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

#### Display
- `category` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — layer category for grouping in UI (e.g., 'Biodiversity', 'Land Cover', 'Climate', 'Infrastructure')
- `displayOrder` (integer, minimum: 0) — ordering priority for display
- `isDefault` (boolean) — whether this layer should be shown by default
- `opacity` (string, maxGraphemes: 8) — default opacity (0-1 as string, e.g., '0.7')
- `thumbnail` (ref to app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail) — preview thumbnail

#### Legend
- `legend` (array of objects, maxLength: 20) — legend entries. Each has `label` (string, maxGraphemes: 128), `color` (string, maxGraphemes: 16, e.g., '#FF5733'), and optional `value` (string, maxGraphemes: 64)
- `colorScale` (string, maxGraphemes: 64, knownValues: ['viridis', 'plasma', 'inferno', 'magma', 'cividis', 'turbo', 'spectral', 'rdylgn', 'rdylbu', 'custom']) — named color scale for continuous data
- `unit` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — unit of measurement for the layer data (e.g., 'species/ha', 'kg C/m²', 'mm/year')
- `minValue` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — minimum value in the data range
- `maxValue` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — maximum value in the data range

#### Tile/Raster specific
- `tilePattern` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — URL pattern for TMS tiles (e.g., 'https://tiles.example.com/{z}/{x}/{y}.png')
- `tileMinZoom` (integer, minimum: 0, maximum: 22) — minimum zoom level
- `tileMaxZoom` (integer, minimum: 0, maximum: 22) — maximum zoom level
- `bounds` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — bounding box as 'west,south,east,north'

#### Data source
- `dataSource` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — attribution/source of the layer data
- `dataDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — date of the data (ISO 8601)
- `propertyKey` (string, maxGraphemes: 128) — GeoJSON property key to use for choropleth coloring (e.g., 'species_richness')
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the site this layer belongs to

### Also add new layer type values to the existing type enum:
Add these to the existing enum array: 'heatmap', 'contour', 'satellite_overlay'

### Define legendEntry as a separate def called `legendEntry` in the same file's defs section.

## Don't
- Do NOT remove any existing enum values from type
- Do NOT change any existing required fields
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT add any new required fields
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. All existing required fields unchanged (name, type, uri, createdAt)
3. Existing type enum values preserved, new values added
4. All new fields are optional
5. legendEntry def properly defined in defs section
6. legend items reference the legendEntry def
7. thumbnail refs app.gainforest.common.defs#imageThumbnail
8. siteRef has format: at-uri
9. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
10. All integer fields have appropriate minimum/maximum

title: Enrich DwC occurrence lexicon with GainForest-specific conservation fields
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.json (modify)

## What to do
The DwC occurrence record is already comprehensive for standard biodiversity data. Add optional fields specific to GainForest's conservation monitoring and data analytics needs. These fields capture data the Green Globe app currently fetches from AWS S3 (Restor plant predictions, animal CSVs, measured tree GeoJSON).

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

#### Conservation Status
- `iucnCategory` (string, maxGraphemes: 16, knownValues: ['EX', 'EW', 'CR', 'EN', 'VU', 'NT', 'LC', 'DD', 'NE']) — IUCN Red List category
- `iucnTaxonId` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — IUCN Red List taxon ID
- `iucnAssessmentDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — date of IUCN assessment (ISO 8601)
- `nativeStatus` (string, maxGraphemes: 32, knownValues: ['native', 'invasive', 'introduced', 'endemic', 'uncertain']) — whether species is native/invasive at this location
- `citesAppendix` (string, maxGraphemes: 8, knownValues: ['I', 'II', 'III']) — CITES appendix listing
- `nationalConservationStatus` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — national/regional conservation status

#### Plant Traits (for flora occurrences — from Restor/TRY database)
- `woodDensity` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — wood density in g/cm³
- `treeMaxHeight` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — maximum tree height in meters
- `stemDiameter` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — typical stem diameter in cm
- `stemConduitDiameter` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — stem conduit diameter in μm
- `barkThickness` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — bark thickness in mm
- `rootDepth` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — root depth in meters
- `edibleParts` (array of strings, maxLength: 10, each maxGraphemes: 64) — edible parts (e.g., 'fruit', 'leaves', 'seeds', 'bark', 'roots')
- `economicUses` (array of strings, maxLength: 10, each maxGraphemes: 128) — economic uses (e.g., 'timber', 'medicine', 'food', 'fiber', 'dye')

#### Monitoring Provenance
- `projectRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the organization info record
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the site record where this was observed
- `monitoringProgramme` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — name of the monitoring programme
- `datasetRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to a dataset record this occurrence belongs to

#### Display Helpers
- `thumbnailUrl` (string, format: uri) — URL to a thumbnail image for display in lists
- `speciesImageUrl` (string, format: uri) — URL to a representative species image

## Don't
- Do NOT change any existing required fields (basisOfRecord, scientificName, eventDate, createdAt)
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT remove or modify any existing properties
- Do NOT add any new required fields
- Do NOT duplicate fields that already exist (e.g., don't add another scientificName)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. All existing required fields unchanged: basisOfRecord, scientificName, eventDate, createdAt
3. All existing properties preserved exactly as-is
4. All new fields are optional (not in required array)
5. iucnCategory uses knownValues (not enum) with correct IUCN codes
6. nativeStatus uses knownValues (not enum)
7. citesAppendix uses knownValues (not enum)
8. projectRef and siteRef have format: at-uri
9. thumbnailUrl and speciesImageUrl have format: uri
10. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints
11. edibleParts and economicUses are arrays with maxLength and item maxGraphemes
close_reason: b562e90 Enrich DwC occurrence lexicon with GainForest-specific conservation fields

title: Enrich common defs with conservation and taxonomy helper types
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/common/defs.json (modify)

## What to do
Add new shared type definitions that will be referenced by multiple lexicons. These capture patterns used across the GainForest platform.

### New defs to add (all as new entries in the existing defs object):

#### `conservationStatus`
An object capturing IUCN and other conservation status info. Required: none. Properties:
- `iucnCategory` (string, maxGraphemes: 16, knownValues: ['EX', 'EW', 'CR', 'EN', 'VU', 'NT', 'LC', 'DD', 'NE'])
- `iucnTaxonId` (string, maxGraphemes: 32)
- `iucnAssessmentDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64)
- `citesAppendix` (string, maxGraphemes: 8, knownValues: ['I', 'II', 'III'])
- `nationalStatus` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — national/regional status
- `nativeStatus` (string, maxGraphemes: 32, knownValues: ['native', 'invasive', 'introduced', 'endemic', 'uncertain'])

#### `speciesProfile`
A display-ready species profile for UI rendering. Required: `scientificName`. Properties:
- `scientificName` (string, maxGraphemes: 512)
- `commonName` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — vernacular name
- `gbifTaxonKey` (string, maxGraphemes: 64)
- `kingdom` (string, maxGraphemes: 128)
- `family` (string, maxGraphemes: 128)
- `genus` (string, maxGraphemes: 128)
- `imageUrl` (string, format: uri) — representative species image
- `thumbnailUrl` (string, format: uri) — small thumbnail
- `conservationStatus` (ref to #conservationStatus)

#### `coordinatePair`
A simple lat/lon pair for lightweight location references. Required: `lat`, `lon`. Properties:
- `lat` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — decimal latitude
- `lon` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — decimal longitude

#### `dateRange`
A date range for temporal queries. Required: `startDate`. Properties:
- `startDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — ISO 8601 start date
- `endDate` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — ISO 8601 end date

#### `externalReference`
A reference to an external system/database. Required: `system`, `identifier`. Properties:
- `system` (string, maxGraphemes: 128, knownValues: ['gbif', 'iucn', 'ncbi', 'bold', 'ebird', 'inaturalist', 'wdpa', 'restor', 'other']) — external system name
- `identifier` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — identifier in that system
- `url` (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 512) — direct URL to the record

## Don't
- Do NOT modify any existing defs (richtext, uri, image, imageThumbnail, video, audio, spectrogram, document, dataFile, gpsTrack, geospatial, sensorData, geneticData, indexedOrganization)
- Do NOT change the lexicon id
- Do NOT change the description of the file
acceptance_criteria:
1. File is valid JSON and valid ATProto lexicon schema
2. All 13 existing defs are completely unchanged
3. 5 new defs added: conservationStatus, speciesProfile, coordinatePair, dateRange, externalReference
4. conservationStatus has no required fields
5. speciesProfile requires only scientificName
6. coordinatePair requires lat and lon
7. dateRange requires only startDate
8. externalReference requires system and identifier
9. speciesProfile.conservationStatus refs #conservationStatus correctly
10. All knownValues used (not enum) for extensible lists
11. All string fields have maxGraphemes constraints

## What to do
Add new shared type definitions to the Darwin Core defs that support GainForest's plant monitoring and abundance tracking workflows.

title: Enrich DwC event lexicon with GainForest monitoring context
description:
## Files
- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/event.json (modify)

## What to do
Add optional fields to the DwC event record that support GainForest's specific monitoring workflows — linking events to organization sites, tracking environmental conditions during surveys, and supporting repeat monitoring.

### New OPTIONAL properties to add to the main record:

#### GainForest Linkage
- `projectRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the organization info record
- `siteRef` (string, format: at-uri) — AT-URI reference to the organization site record
- `monitoringProgramme` (string, maxGraphemes: 256) — name of the monitoring programme (e.g., 'Annual Biodiversity Survey 2025')
- `monitoringFrequency` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — how often this type of event recurs (e.g., 'monthly', 'quarterly', 'annual', 'one-time')

#### Environmental Conditions During Event
- `temperature` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — temperature in Celsius during event
- `humidity` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — relative humidity percentage
- `windSpeed` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — wind speed during event
- `cloudCover` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — cloud cover percentage
- `precipitation` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — precipitation description (e.g., 'none', 'light rain', '5mm')
- `weatherRemarks` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — general weather description
- `moonPhase` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — moon phase (relevant for nocturnal surveys)
- `waterLevel` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — water level if aquatic survey (e.g., 'low', '2.3m')
- `waterTemperature` (string, maxGraphemes: 16) — water temperature in Celsius
- `visibility` (string, maxGraphemes: 32) — visibility conditions (e.g., 'clear', 'foggy', '10m underwater')

#### Team & Equipment
- `teamSize` (integer, minimum: 1) — number of people involved in the event
- `recordedBy` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — person(s) who conducted the event (pipe-delimited)
- `recordedByID` (string, maxGraphemes: 512) — ORCID or persistent IDs (pipe-delimited)
- `equipmentUsed` (string, maxGraphemes: 1024) — description of equipment used

#### Data Quality
- `qualityControlNotes` (string, maxGraphemes: 2048) — notes on data quality issues
- `completeness` (string, maxGraphemes: 64) — assessment of survey completeness (e.g., 'complete', 'partial - rain stopped survey', 'incomplete')

## Don't

title: Fix: Task 8.1 made breaking changes to lexicon JSONs that should be read-only (from green_globe-8.1)
description:
Review of green_globe-8.1 found: The task spec explicitly stated 'Do NOT modify any lexicon JSON files — they are the source of truth from Epic 1', but the commit (35ba769) modified 3 lexicon JSON files:

1. **donation.json** (breaking): Changed `amount` field from `type: string` to `type: integer` and added `donorIdentifier` as a new required field. Epic 1 task green_globe-1.10 created this lexicon with `amount` as a string and without `donorIdentifier` in required. This is a breaking schema change that was not reviewed or approved.

2. **member.json** (breaking): Added `did` to the required array. Epic 1 task green_globe-1.4 created this lexicon with `required: [displayName, role, createdAt]`. Adding `did` to required is a breaking change — any existing records without `did` will now fail validation.

3. **info.json** (schema pollution): Added a new `speciesFocus` field that is nearly identical to the existing `focusSpeciesGroups` field (same knownValues array, same description intent). This creates a redundant duplicate field in the schema. The `speciesFocus` field was added to satisfy AC3 of task 8.1 ('info.ts contains speciesFocus'), but the correct fix was to update the acceptance criteria to reference `focusSpeciesGroups` (which already existed from Epic 1 task 1.14), not to add a new field.

Evidence: `git show 35ba769 -- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/donation.json` shows the amount type change and donorIdentifier addition. `git show 35ba769 -- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/member.json` shows did added to required.

Required fix: Revert the lexicon JSON changes to match what Epic 1 produced. Update the acceptance criteria of any future task to reference the correct field names (`focusSpeciesGroups` not `speciesFocus`, `amount` as string not integer). The generated TypeScript types should then be regenerated from the corrected lexicons.
close_reason: 35ba769 Accepted: schema changes to new lexicons with no existing PDS records

title: Fix: Task 8.3 modified read-only src/ files and tsconfig.json (from green_globe-8.3)
description:
Review of green_globe-8.3 found: The task spec explicitly listed src/ files as read-only ('verify imports still work') and said 'Do NOT modify any source (.tsx/.ts) files in src/'. The commit (bedd8ef) modified two src/ files and tsconfig.json:

1. **src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/index.tsx**: Removed the `import { BlobRef } from '@atproto/api'` import and changed `coverImage.ref` to `coverImage.image.ref`. The old code cast coverImage as BlobRef and accessed .ref directly; the new code accesses .image.ref because the type changed from BlobRef to OrgHypercertsDefs.SmallImage (which has an `image: BlobRef` field). The type change is correct but the file was supposed to be read-only.

2. **src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/ProjectInfo.tsx**: Changed `organization.longDescription` rendering from direct string interpolation to a runtime type guard (`typeof organization.longDescription === 'string'`). The longDescription field is now typed as `PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main` (a complex rich-text document object), not a string. The type guard silently renders nothing when longDescription is a document object — this is a functional regression. The correct fix would be to render the LinearDocument properly or file a separate issue for it.

3. **tsconfig.json**: Added `climateai-sdk-scalability` and `bumicerts-platform-staging` to the exclude array. While this may have been necessary to prevent TypeScript from picking up the SDK's conflicting types, it was not in scope for this task and was not mentioned in the task description.

Evidence: `git show bedd8ef -- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/index.tsx` and `git show bedd8ef -- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/ProjectInfo.tsx` show the changes.

The longDescription regression is the most serious issue: organizations with rich-text long descriptions will show nothing in the ProjectInfo panel. A follow-up task should properly render PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument content.
close_reason: bedd8ef Accepted: src/ changes were necessary for build to pass

title: Fix: Non-gainforest lexicon JSONs added to project lexicons/ directory (from green_globe-8.3)
description:
Review of green_globe-8.3 found: The task spec for green_globe-8.2 explicitly stated 'Do NOT copy non-gainforest lexicons (com/, org/, pub/, app/bsky/) — the project only needs the gainforest namespace'. However, task 8.3 added 19 non-gainforest lexicon JSON files to the project's lexicons/ directory as a side effect of running codegen:

- lexicons/app/bsky/richtext/facet.json
- lexicons/com/atproto/repo/strongRef.json
- lexicons/org/hypercerts/defs.json
- lexicons/pub/leaflet/blocks/*.json (13 files)
- lexicons/pub/leaflet/pages/linearDocument.json
- lexicons/pub/leaflet/richtext/facet.json

These were added because the gainforest lexicons reference types from these namespaces (e.g., info.json references pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument and org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage), and the codegen tool needed them to resolve cross-references.

The issue is that these are now committed to the project repository as source files, creating maintenance burden: they will need to be kept in sync with the upstream lexicons they came from, and they were not part of the intended scope.

Evidence: `git show bedd8ef --stat` shows 19 non-gainforest lexicon files added. The task 8.2 spec at green_globe-8.2 explicitly prohibited this.

Recommended fix: Either (a) add these files to .gitignore as generated/dependency files, or (b) document them as intentional dependencies of the gainforest lexicons and add a note to the README explaining they must be kept in sync with upstream.
close_reason: bedd8ef Accepted: cross-reference lexicons required for codegen

title: Fix: longDescription rendered as nothing when it is a LinearDocument object (from green_globe-8.3)
description:
Review of green_globe-8.3 found a functional regression in ProjectInfo.tsx: the longDescription field was previously typed as string and rendered directly. After the lexicon regeneration, it is now typed as PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main (a complex rich-text document object with a blocks array).

The worker patched this with a runtime type guard:
```tsx
{typeof organization.longDescription === 'string'
  ? organization.longDescription
  : null}
```

This means: when longDescription is a LinearDocument object (which is what the lexicon defines it as), the component renders nothing. Any organization that has stored their longDescription as a LinearDocument will see a blank description panel.

The correct fix is to either:
(a) Implement a LinearDocument renderer component that renders the blocks array into React elements, or
(b) If the app currently stores longDescription as a plain string in practice (legacy data), add a migration path and render the string content from within the document structure

This is a functional regression introduced by the type change in the lexicon. The task 8.3 spec said 'Do NOT modify any source (.tsx/.ts) files in src/' — the worker modified the file but introduced a silent failure mode instead of a proper fix.

Evidence: `git show bedd8ef -- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/ProjectInfo.tsx` shows the type guard. The lexicon-api/types/app/gainforest/organization/info.ts line 25 shows `longDescription: PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main`.
close_reason: d1e5e3a LinearDocumentRenderer component created in task 8.4

title: Fix: speciesFocus field in info.json is a near-duplicate of focusSpeciesGroups — schema redundancy (from green_globe-8.1)
description:
Review of green_globe-8.1 found: The commit (35ba769) added a new `speciesFocus` field to climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/info.json. This field is nearly identical to the existing `focusSpeciesGroups` field that was added by Epic 1 task green_globe-1.14:

- Both fields are optional arrays of strings
- Both have maxLength: 20 and maxGraphemes: 64 per item
- Both have identical knownValues arrays (mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, fish, insects, arachnids, mollusks, crustaceans, trees, shrubs, herbs, grasses, ferns, mosses, fungi, algae, coral, other)
- The only difference is the description: speciesFocus says 'primary field', focusSpeciesGroups says 'Species groups the organization focuses on'

This creates schema redundancy. Clients will not know which field to use. The `speciesFocus` field was added to satisfy task 8.1 AC3 ('info.ts contains speciesFocus'), but the acceptance criteria was wrong — it should have referenced `focusSpeciesGroups` which already existed.

Evidence: `git show 35ba769 -- climateai-sdk-scalability/lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/info.json` shows speciesFocus added. The existing focusSpeciesGroups field is visible in the same file at line 177.

Recommended fix: Remove `speciesFocus` from info.json (it is a duplicate). Update any documentation or acceptance criteria that references `speciesFocus` to use `focusSpeciesGroups` instead. Regenerate the TypeScript types after removing the duplicate field.
close_reason: a58dac2 speciesFocus removed in task 8.5

title: Epic: Integrate gainforest-sdk and establish tRPC foundation
description:
## Why
The Green Globe app currently uses raw @atproto/api calls with inconsistent Agent creation patterns (some use a shared singleton, some create new instances inline), hardcoded collection strings, and manual type casting. The gainforest-sdk provides a batteries-included tRPC SDK with type-safe CRUD operations, blob URL generation, AT URI parsing, serialization utilities, and a complete tRPC router that eliminates the need for hand-rolled API routes.

## What Success Looks Like
- gainforest-sdk is installed and configured (vendor tarball from bumicerts-platform-staging/vendor/)
- tRPC router is mounted at /api/trpc with the SDK's appRouter
- A tRPC React client (trpcApi) is available for client-side queries/mutations
- Server-side caller (getServerCaller) is available for Server Components
- The existing ATProto OAuth provider is adapted to work with the SDK's auth system OR the SDK's OAuth is integrated
- SDK utilities (getBlobUrl, parseAtUri, serialize/deserialize) are available project-wide
- The existing /api/list-organizations and /api/shapefiles routes still work (not yet replaced, just coexisting)
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

## Key Constraints
- The SDK tarball is at bumicerts-platform-staging/vendor/gainforest-sdk-nextjs-0.1.1.tgz — copy to vendor/ in this project
- The SDK needs a PDS domain config (climateai.org for staging)
- The SDK's OAuth uses Supabase for session/state storage — we need to evaluate if we keep the existing BrowserOAuthClient approach or migrate to the SDK's OAuth. For this epic, we will keep the existing OAuth and only set up the SDK for read operations (no auth required for reads).
- Reference bumicerts-platform-staging/ for all patterns
- Do NOT remove any existing API routes or data fetching — this epic is additive only
close_reason: eb7e61f All 5 tasks complete: SDK installed, configured, tRPC mounted, utils exported, smoke tested

title: Epic: Replace Airtable organization index with SDK and HyperIndex
description:
## Why
The organization index (which organizations exist and are approved) currently comes from Airtable. This is the single biggest non-ATProto dependency for the core map experience. The /api/list-organizations route fetches DIDs from Airtable, then enriches each DID with info and coordinates from the PDS. This is slow (N+1 queries), fragile (Airtable API key dependency), and not decentralized.

## Approach: PDS listRepos + SDK batch (Option B)
HyperIndex does NOT index app.gainforest.organization.* collections (confirmed via live query — 0 results). It only indexes app.gainforest.dwc.*, app.certified.*, org.hypercerts.*, etc.

Instead, we use the same pattern the SDK uses internally for getAllAcrossOrgs:
1. Call com.atproto.sync.listRepos on the PDS (climateai.org) to get all ~98 repos
2. For each repo, call gainforest.organization.info.get({ did, pdsDomain }) via SDK server caller
3. Filter to visibility === 'Public'
4. For each public org, call hypercerts.location.getDefault({ did, pdsDomain }) via SDK to get coordinates
5. All calls parallelized with Promise.all — ~98 repos is fine

This is exactly how the SDK's hypercerts.claim.activity.getAllAcrossOrgs works internally (confirmed by reading the SDK source).

## What Success Looks Like
- The /api/list-organizations route no longer depends on Airtable
- Organization data (DIDs, names, countries, coordinates) comes from PDS via SDK tRPC server caller
- The useIndexedOrganizations hook returns the same data shape as before
- Map markers still render correctly with organization names and coordinates
- The search/filter functionality in the sidebar still works
- Airtable env vars (AIRTABLE_GREEN_GLOBE_TOKEN, AIRTABLE_GREEN_GLOBE_ORGS_BASE_ID) can be removed
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

## Key Constraints
- PDS endpoint: https://climateai.org (98 active repos, 76 from migration + 22 new)
- SDK's info.get uses collection 'app.gainforest.organization.info' with rkey 'self'
- SDK's location routes use 'app.certified.location' (NOT app.gainforest.organization.site)
- SDK's location.getDefault fetches 'app.gainforest.organization.defaultSite' then resolves to certified location
- The current route returns: { did, name, country, lat, lon } per organization
- Organizations must be filtered to only 'Public' visibility
- Some repos are test accounts (tester-tester, satyam-tester, etc.) — they won't have org info records, so info.get will fail for them. Handle gracefully with try/catch.
- Reference: tmp/pds-repo-inventory-20251118T0945-neworgs.json for the full repo list

- ?info=true — include name and country
- ?mapPoint=true — include lat and lon

title: Epic: Replace legacy GraphQL API with ATProto site records for project detail
description:
## Why
The project detail page (ProjectOverlay) fetches data from a legacy GainForest GraphQL API (NEXT_PUBLIC_GAINFOREST_ENDPOINT/api/graphql) and a supplementary backend API (NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_API_URL). Analysis shows that most GraphQL fields are already migrated to ATProto and NOT consumed by the UI anymore. Only 3 fields are actually used:

1. **name** — used to construct AWS S3 paths (kebab-cased) for trees, biodiversity, layers
2. **assets[]** — used for site selection (filtering to classification='Shapefiles'), polygon loading (awsCID), and default site selection
3. **communityMembers[]** — used only by the Donations feature for wallet address lookup (Donations tab is currently disabled/commented out)

The ATProto PDS already has organization.site records with name, lat, lon, area, and shapefile blobs. The site selector can be rebuilt to list ATProto site records instead of filtering GraphQL assets[].

## What Success Looks Like
- The ProjectOverlay store no longer calls the legacy GraphQL API
- The ProjectOverlay store no longer calls the backend API
- Site selection uses ATProto organization.site records (via SDK tRPC or direct PDS calls)
- Polygon/shapefile loading uses ATProto blob refs instead of AWS S3 awsCID paths
- The project name for S3 path construction is derived from the ATProto handle (org-slug.climateai.org -> org-slug) instead of the GraphQL name field
- NEXT_PUBLIC_GAINFOREST_ENDPOINT and NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_API_URL env vars are no longer needed
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

## Key Constraints
- The site records on the PDS have: name, lat, lon, area, shapefile (blob ref or URI), trees (blob ref), coverImage
- The existing /api/shapefiles route already knows how to fetch site records and extract GeoJSON from blobs — reuse this logic
- The organization handle (e.g., kayapo-project.climateai.org) can serve as the slug for S3 path construction: handle.split('.')[0] gives the kebab-case name
- The Donations tab is currently disabled in the UI — communityMembers[] dependency can be deferred to Epic 4 (community members migration)
- Do NOT break the biodiversity or layers features — they still need the project name for S3 paths until those are migrated in later epics

title: Create useOrganizationSites hook to fetch site records from ATProto
description:
## Files
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-sites.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a React Query hook that fetches all site records for a given organization DID from ATProto. This replaces the assets[] array from the legacy GraphQL API.

The hook should:
1. Accept a DID string parameter
2. Use the SDK's tRPC client (trpcApi from trpc-provider.tsx) to call: trpcApi.gainforest.organization.site.getAll.useQuery({ did, pdsDomain: allowedPDSDomains[0] }) — OR if the SDK doesn't have a site.getAll, use the existing /api/shapefiles?dids=<did> route which already lists site records
3. Also fetch the defaultSite record to know which site is the default: trpcApi.gainforest.organization.defaultSite.get.useQuery({ did, pdsDomain: allowedPDSDomains[0] }) — OR use the raw PDS agent to call getRecord for defaultSite
4. Return: { sites: SiteRecord[], defaultSiteUri: string | null, isLoading, error }

Define the SiteRecord type based on what the PDS returns:
```ts
type SiteRecord = {
  uri: string;       // AT-URI (at://did/collection/rkey)
  rkey: string;      // Record key (TID)
  name: string;      // Site name (equivalent to shapefile.shortName)
  lat: string;       // Latitude as decimal string
  lon: string;       // Longitude as decimal string
  area: string;      // Area in hectares as decimal string
  shapefile: unknown; // Blob ref or URI for the GeoJSON boundary
  trees?: unknown;    // Blob ref for tree planting data
  createdAt?: string;
};
```

If using the SDK tRPC, check bumicerts-platform-staging for the exact call pattern. If the SDK doesn't expose site.getAll, fall back to using the existing ClimateAIAgent from src/lib/atproto/agent.ts with com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ repo: did, collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.site' }).

## Don't
- Modify the existing ProjectOverlay store yet (that's a separate task)
- Remove any existing hooks
- Fetch from the legacy GraphQL API
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-sites.ts exports a useOrganizationSites hook
2. The hook accepts a DID and returns { sites, defaultSiteUri, isLoading, error }
3. Sites are fetched from ATProto (PDS or SDK tRPC), not from GraphQL
4. The hook handles the case where a DID has no sites (returns empty array)
5. bun run lint passes

title: Epic: Migrate biodiversity data display to ATProto records
description:
## Why
The Biodiversity tab currently fetches all data from static files on AWS S3:
- Plants (trees + herbs): JSON files from Restor API cached at {AWS_STORAGE}/restor/{slug}-trees.json and -herbs.json
- Animals: CSV files at {AWS_STORAGE}/predictions/{slug}.csv
- Measured trees: GeoJSON at {AWS_STORAGE}/shapefiles/{slug}-all-tree-plantings.geojson

The ATProto PDS already has:
- organization.predictions.flora/fauna records (with gbifTaxonKeys arrays) — migrated in Phase 10
- organization.observations.measuredTreesCluster lexicon defined
- Trees blob data already stored on site records (trees blob ref)
- DwC occurrence/event/measurement lexicons for proper biodiversity records

However, the current predictions records only store GBIF taxon key arrays, not the rich species data (traits, images, IUCN status) that the UI displays. The Restor JSON files contain much richer data. This epic focuses on making the frontend read from ATProto where possible and gracefully falling back to S3 where the data isn't rich enough yet.

## What Success Looks Like
- Measured trees GeoJSON is loaded from the ATProto site record's trees blob ref instead of S3
- Predictions data has a clear path: either read from ATProto records (if enriched) or continue reading from S3 with the slug derived from ATProto handle (not GraphQL name)
- The S3 path construction uses the ATProto handle slug (already done in Epic 3)
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

## Key Constraints
- The predictions.flora/fauna records on PDS only have gbifTaxonKeys — NOT the rich Restor data (traits, images, IUCN)
- The measured trees GeoJSON is already stored as a blob on site records (trees field)
- The tree photos are still on S3 at /trees-measured/{treeID}.jpg — these won't move to ATProto
- The Restor JSON format is complex (species with traits, images, IUCN categories) — full migration to DwC occurrence records is a larger effort
- For this epic, focus on: (1) trees from PDS blobs, (2) keeping S3 fallback for predictions with handle-based slugs

title: Epic: Migrate map layers configuration to ATProto layer records
description:
## Why
Map layer configuration is currently stored as static JSON files on AWS S3:
- Global layers: {AWS_STORAGE}/layers/global/layerData.json
- Project-specific layers: {AWS_STORAGE}/layers/{slug}/layerData.json

Each layerData.json contains an array of layer objects with: name, type (geojson_points, raster_tif, choropleth, etc.), endpoint (relative S3 path), category, visible, and optional legend/styling info.

The ATProto organization.layer lexicon is already defined with fields for: name, type, uri, category, description, legend, styling, visibility, and more. Layer records should be stored per-organization on the PDS, and the frontend should read them from ATProto instead of S3 JSON files.

Note: The actual layer DATA (GeoJSON files, COG rasters) will likely remain on S3/external storage — only the layer CONFIGURATION/METADATA moves to ATProto. The layer record's uri field will point to the S3 data URL.

## What Success Looks Like
- Layer configuration is read from ATProto organization.layer records (via SDK tRPC or direct PDS calls)
- Global layers are stored on a designated 'global' organization account (or a shared config)
- Project-specific layers are stored on each organization's PDS repo
- The LayersOverlay component renders layers from ATProto data
- Dynamic layer rendering (points, lines, choropleth, raster) still works (data URLs in layer records point to S3)
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

## Key Constraints
- Layer DATA stays on S3 — only the configuration/metadata moves to ATProto
- The layer record's uri field stores the full S3 URL to the data file
- Global layers need a home — either a dedicated PDS account or a different approach
- The TiTiler integration for raster layers needs the S3 URL — this stays the same
- Migration script needed to convert layerData.json files into ATProto layer records

3. Transform the ATProto layer records into the DynamicLayer shape that the LayersOverlay store expects
4. Return: { layers: DynamicLayer[], isLoading, error }

The DynamicLayer type (from the existing LayersOverlay store) has:
```ts
type DynamicLayer = {
  name: string;
  type: string; // geojson_points, raster_tif, choropleth, etc.
  endpoint: string; // S3 path or full URL
  category?: string;
  visible?: boolean;
  legend?: unknown;
};
```

Map ATProto layer record fields to DynamicLayer:
- layer.name -> name
- layer.type -> type
- layer.uri -> endpoint (the S3 URL to the data)
- layer.category -> category
- layer.visibility -> visible

## Don't

- Fetch from S3 layerData.json
- Handle global layers (that's a separate concern)

title: Migrate PDS shortDescription fields from plain string to richtext object
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js (create)
- scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js (create)

## What to do

### Problem
All existing PDS records on climateai.org have shortDescription as a plain string (e.g. 'FLORESTAL SANTA MARIA PROJECT...'). The SDK lexicon v0.1.1 expects shortDescription to be a richtext object: { text: 'the string', facets: [] } matching the app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext schema. This causes gainforestSdk.getServerCaller().gainforest.organization.info.get() to throw UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT for every organization.

### 1. Create check script: scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/pds/check-info-descriptions.js but instead of checking for escaped newlines, check the TYPE of shortDescription:
- For each repo, fetch the info record
- Report whether shortDescription is a string (needs migration) or an object (already migrated)
- Output results to tmp/info-shortdescription-type-check.json

### 2. Create migration script: scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/migrations/normalize-info-descriptions.js (same CLI args, same loadInventory, same loadCredentials, same login flow, same dry-run support, same result logging). The ONLY difference is the transformation logic:

For each repo's info record:
1. Read the current shortDescription value
2. If it's already an object with a 'text' property, skip (already migrated)
3. If it's a string, transform it to: { text: shortDescription } (no facets needed — plain text has no rich formatting)
4. Write the updated record back with putRecord (preserving ALL other fields unchanged)

CLI interface (identical to existing migration scripts):
```
node scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js   [--service https://climateai.org]   [--repos-file tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json]   [--credentials tmp/pds-credentials.json]   [--limit N]   [--handle handle.climateai.org]   [--dry-run]   [--out tmp/info-shortdescription-richtext-migration-results.json]
```

### Key details
- The richtext schema is: { text: string, facets?: array } — only 'text' is required
- Do NOT add $type to the richtext object — ATProto putRecord handles that
- Preserve ALL other fields on the info record (spread the existing record, only replace shortDescription)
- Use CommonJS require() style like all other migration scripts (not ESM import)
- Use the same eslint-disable comments as existing scripts

## Don't
- Modify longDescription (it uses a different schema — linearDocument — and is a separate concern)
- Modify any existing migration scripts
- Modify any application source code
- Run the migration (just create the scripts — running is a manual step)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js exists and follows the pattern of check-info-descriptions.js
2. scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js exists and follows the pattern of normalize-info-descriptions.js
3. The migration transforms string shortDescription to { text: shortDescription } richtext object
4. Already-migrated records (shortDescription is object with text property) are skipped
5. All other info record fields are preserved unchanged during migration
6. Both scripts support --dry-run, --limit, --handle, --service, --repos-file, --credentials, --out flags
7. bun run lint passes
8. bun run build passes
close_reason: eb7e61f add shortDescription richtext migration and check scripts

title: Fix: occurrence.json duplicates conservationStatus fields inline instead of referencing common.defs#conservationStatus (from green_globe-1.6 / green_globe-1.7)
description: Review of green_globe-1.6 and green_globe-1.7 found a cross-task seam problem. Task 1.7 defined a shared conservationStatus def in common/defs.json with fields: iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, iucnAssessmentDate, citesAppendix, nationalStatus, nativeStatus. Task 1.6 independently added the same conservation fields inline directly on occurrence.json (iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, iucnAssessmentDate, nativeStatus, citesAppendix, nationalConservationStatus) instead of referencing the shared def. This creates two problems: (1) 5 fields are duplicated between occurrence.json inline and common.defs#conservationStatus, wasting the shared def that task 1.7 created; (2) naming inconsistency — occurrence.json uses 'nationalConservationStatus' but common.defs#conservationStatus uses 'nationalStatus' for the same concept. The occurrence.json should either reference #conservationStatus via a ref, or the common def should be updated to match. This will cause confusion for SDK consumers and lexgen-generated types.

title: Fix: audio.json metadata fields missing maxGraphemes and integer minimum constraints (from green_globe-1.3)
description: Review of green_globe-1.3 found multiple missing constraints in organization/recordings/audio.json. (1) String fields missing maxGraphemes: metadata.codec (no maxGraphemes, no format), metadata.duration (no maxGraphemes, no format), metadata.coordinates (no maxGraphemes, no format — this is a pre-existing field but the task touched the file and should have added it), main.name (no maxGraphemes, no format). (2) Integer fields missing minimum constraints: metadata.channels has no minimum (should be minimum: 1), metadata.sampleRate has no minimum (should be minimum: 1 or minimum: 0). These are pre-existing fields that the task did not fix, but AC9 and AC10 require 'all string fields have maxGraphemes constraints' and 'all integer fields have appropriate minimum/maximum'. The new fields added by the task (bitDepth, fileSizeBytes, minFrequencyHz, maxFrequencyHz) do have correct constraints.

title: Fix: layer.json name and description fields missing maxGraphemes (from green_globe-1.5)
description: Review of green_globe-1.5 found that organization/layer.json has two pre-existing string fields without maxGraphemes constraints: (1) 'name' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format — the spec says this is the layer name but no length constraint was added. (2) 'description' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format. AC9 requires 'all string fields have maxGraphemes constraints'. These are pre-existing fields that the task did not fix. All new fields added by the task (category, opacity, unit, minValue, maxValue, tilePattern, bounds, dataSource, dataDate, propertyKey) do have correct maxGraphemes constraints. The legendEntry def fields also have correct constraints.

title: Fix: occurrence.json and dwc/defs.json plantTraits duplicate plant trait fields (from green_globe-1.6 / green_globe-1.8)
description: Review of green_globe-1.6 and green_globe-1.8 found a second cross-task duplication. Task 1.8 defined a plantTraits def in dwc/defs.json with fields: woodDensity, maxHeight, stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, leafArea, specificLeafArea, seedMass, growthForm, leafType, dispersalMode, pollinationMode, edibleParts, economicUses, traitSource. Task 1.6 independently added plant trait fields inline on occurrence.json: woodDensity, treeMaxHeight (note: different name from plantTraits.maxHeight), stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, edibleParts, economicUses. This creates: (1) duplication of 6 fields between occurrence.json inline and dwc.defs#plantTraits; (2) naming inconsistency — occurrence.json uses 'treeMaxHeight' but dwc.defs#plantTraits uses 'maxHeight' for the same concept. The occurrence.json should reference dwc.defs#plantTraits via a ref field, or the shared def should be removed. This will cause confusion for SDK consumers.

title: Fix: info.json email field spec says format:uri which is semantically incorrect for email addresses (from green_globe-1.1)
description: Review of green_globe-1.1 spec found a semantic error in the task description itself: the spec says email should have 'format: uri' (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 256). Email addresses are NOT URIs — they are not valid URI format values in ATProto's lexicon system. The correct approach is either: (a) use type: string with maxGraphemes: 256 and no format (email is just a string), or (b) use format: 'email' if ATProto supports it. Using format: uri would cause validation failures for any email address that doesn't start with a URI scheme. This is a spec defect that needs to be corrected when task 1.1 is re-implemented. Note: this issue is secondary to green_globe-2 (the fields were never added at all), but the spec itself needs fixing before re-implementation.
